creator: avorobyoff@gmail.com
text:
I'm using computer with no keyboard connected.
Computer uses ASUS P5G41T-M SI motherboard (Core2Duo E6300 on G41 chipset). No keyboard connected.
When I try to reboot or shutdown computer it silently reboots after "syslogd: exiting on signal 15" and does not unmount filesystems. So when system boots it warns:
"Nov  7 21:17:38 gate kernel: WARNING: /jail was not properly dismounted
Nov  7 21:17:38 gate kernel: WARNING: /tmp was not properly dismounted
Nov  7 21:17:38 gate kernel: WARNING: /usr was not properly dismounted
Nov  7 21:17:38 gate kernel: WARNING: /var was not properly dismounted
Nov  7 21:17:38 gate kernel: WARNING: /opt was not properly dismounted"

If I connect keyboard (either AT or USB) and try to reboot or shutdown system - everything is OK.

How-To-Repeat: Try to disconnect keyboard and shutdown system (using ssh login for example).
